questions.question_text: A licensee posts a property listing on social media without mentioning the brokerage. Under Rea 404.05, what type of ad is this?
distractors: A compliant digital ad
An exempt personal post
A permissible team ad
questions.explanation: Under Rea 404.05, all advertisements must include the brokerage name as licensed. An ad that omits the brokerage identification is a blind ad and is prohibited regardless of the medium, including social media.

questions.question_text: What must every real estate advertisement in New Hampshire include under Rea 404.05?
distractors: The salesperson's personal name
The MLS listing number
The OPLC license number
questions.explanation: Rea 404.05 requires all real estate advertisements to include the brokerage name exactly as it appears on the license. This applies to all media including print, internet, and social media.

questions.question_text: A real estate team wants to advertise under their team name. Which statement about NH advertising rules is correct?
questions.correct_answer: Team names must include brokerage ID
distractors: Team names fully replace the brokerage ID
Team ads are completely exempt from rules
Teams cannot advertise separately at all
questions.explanation: In New Hampshire, team names are permitted in advertising, but the advertisement must still include the brokerage identification as required by Rea 404.05. The team name does not replace the brokerage name.

questions.question_text: A broker publishes a newspaper ad for a listing. Which element is required for compliance with NH advertising rules?
questions.correct_answer: Brokerage name as it is licensed
distractors: Agent's personal phone number
Listing price and square footage
Commission rate offered to buyers
questions.explanation: Under Rea 404.05, every advertisement must contain the brokerage name as licensed. There is no requirement to include the agent's phone number, property details, or commission information for the ad to be compliant.

questions.question_text: An agent argues that internet ads have different rules than print ads in NH. Is this correct?
questions.correct_answer: No, same rules apply to all media
distractors: Yes, internet ads are less strict
Yes, internet ads need more detail
Yes, social media is fully exempt
questions.explanation: New Hampshire applies the same advertising rules to all media. Internet and social media advertisements must comply with the same Rea 404.05 requirements as print advertisements, including displaying the brokerage name as licensed.

questions.question_text: Under RSA 331-A:16, what is required for a brokerage to operate a branch office in New Hampshire?
distractors: Only a written notice filed to OPLC
Formal approval from the local MLS board
Registration with the municipal town clerk
questions.explanation: RSA 331-A:16 and 16-a require that branch offices be separately licensed. Each branch must also have a managing broker or designated supervisor overseeing operations at that location.

questions.question_text: A principal broker opens a second office location. Who must be assigned to supervise the branch under RSA 331-A:16-a?
distractors: Any active salesperson
The original office manager
A REALTOR association member
questions.explanation: Under RSA 331-A:16-a, each branch office must have a managing broker or a designated supervisor. This person is responsible for oversight of licensees and operations at the branch location.

questions.question_text: A brokerage operates three branch offices. How many branch licenses does NH require?
distractors: One license covers all branches
Two licenses plus main office
No branch license is needed
questions.explanation: Under RSA 331-A:16, each branch office must be separately licensed. Three branch offices require three separate branch office licenses in addition to the main office license.

questions.question_text: Which scenario violates NH branch office requirements?
distractors: Branch with its own separate license
Branch with a designated managing broker
Branch filing its own separate annual reports
questions.explanation: Each branch office must have a managing broker or designated supervisor under RSA 331-A:16-a. Operating a branch without supervisory oversight violates this requirement.

questions.question_text: A salesperson wants to work from a home office and meet clients there regularly. What must the brokerage do under NH law?
questions.correct_answer: License it as a branch office
distractors: File a home office exemption form
Register with the local town office
No action is required by the broker
questions.explanation: If a home office is used regularly to conduct real estate business and meet clients, it functions as a branch office and must be separately licensed under RSA 331-A:16 with appropriate supervision.
